fim_eigenbasis_prior.construction: FIM computed in log-θ space at canonical Zhang θ with this patient's schedule + noise; eigendecomposed; per-direction std = 0.5 if λ_k/λ_max > 1e-3 else 0.05; covariance rotated back to log-θ basis
fim_eigenbasis_prior.interpretation: FIM at this patient's schedule says r_T+/r_TP/α(T-,T+)/α(T-,TP) are well-identified directions (tight prior), r_T- and K_TP_drop are NOT well-identified (loose prior). Note: different from the §3.2 MTD-schedule FIM eigenvectors.

comparison.verdict: None of the three priors fixes NUTS R-hat. All chains diverge into NaN-guard plateaus far from prior mean.

diagnosis: All three NUTS runs (default, hier-informed, FIM-eigenbasis) produce chains that wander FAR outside the prior mean.

Root cause: when proposed θ pushes the integrator into regions producing NaN PSA, the NaN-guard substitutes observed PSA and inflates noise — yielding a FLAT log-likelihood plateau instead of a sharp -inf barrier.
NUTS treats this flat plateau as a valid mode and wanders. Different chains find different plateaus.
Tighter priors (whether marginal or multivariate) cannot fix this because the plateaus are within the high-density region of the prior support.
Two-pronged fix needed: (a) hard-rejection NaN-guard (return -inf log-density), or (b) re-fit canonical θ to Bruchovsky data so prior center is in a data-compatible region.
